Calibrate p-values by a Scalar Genomic-Control Factor

Source: R/calibrate_pvalues.R
calibrate_pvalues.Rd

Recalibrates a vector of p-values by rescaling each on the 1-df chi-square scale by a single calibration_factor, mapping a test whose inflation factor is \(f\) toward \(\lambda = 1\). The same rescaling underlies both classical genomic control and the LD Score regression intercept correction; method selects only where \(f\) comes from.

calibrate_pvalues(
  p,
  method = c("lambda_gc", "ldsc_intercept"),
  calibration_factor = NULL
)

Arguments

p

Numeric vector of p-values.

method

Calibration method – the source of the factor: "lambda_gc" (default) or "ldsc_intercept".

calibration_factor

Optional numeric scalar, the chi-square divisor \(f\). For "lambda_gc" it defaults to compute_lambda_gc(p); for "ldsc_intercept" it is required (the supplied LDSC intercept).

Value

A list with components p (the calibrated vector, same length and order as the input), calibration_factor (the factor used), and method.

Details

On the 1-df chi-square scale the calibration is $$\chi^2_{\mathrm{cal}} = \chi^2 / f, \qquad p_{\mathrm{cal}} = \Pr\!\left(\chi^2_1 > \chi^2_{\mathrm{obs}} / f\right),$$ computed in the upper tail for accuracy at small \(p\). The rescaling is identical across methods; method only selects the source of the factor \(f\):

"lambda_gc"

When calibration_factor is NULL it defaults to compute_lambda_gc(p) (the genomic-control \(\lambda\) estimated from p itself). Here calibration_factor is \(\lambda_{\mathrm{GC}}\), which removes all inflation (confounding and polygenic).

"ldsc_intercept"

calibration_factor is required (the intercept from ldsc_regression); it is not estimable from the p-values being calibrated. The intercept removes confounding while preserving polygenic signal.

A non-finite or non-positive calibration_factor passes p through unchanged (e.g. "lambda_gc" on fewer than 50 usable p-values, where compute_lambda_gc returns NA). NA / out-of-\((0,1]\) entries flow through as NA.

References

Bulik-Sullivan B. et al. (2015). LD Score regression distinguishes confounding from polygenicity in GWAS. Nat Genet 47:291.

See also

compute_lambda_gc, ldsc_regression

Examples

set.seed(1)
chi2 <- rchisq(2000, df = 1) * 1.3                  # an inflated null
p    <- pchisq(chi2, df = 1, lower.tail = FALSE)
compute_lambda_gc(p)                                 # ~1.3
#> [1] 1.329937
cal <- calibrate_pvalues(p, method = "lambda_gc")    # factor estimated from p
compute_lambda_gc(cal$p)                             # ~1.0
#> [1] 1
# LDSC-intercept correction with an externally estimated factor:
calibrate_pvalues(p, method = "ldsc_intercept", calibration_factor = 1.08)$p[1:3]
#> [1] 0.6257382 0.2478162 0.4804427

Contents
